description: User states two distinct details (client name + contract deadline) at turns 0 and 2; 17 user turns of unrelated chat follow; final question asks the assistant to recall both. Tests joint-recall depth pressure: a strategy can sometimes recover one buried fact by guessing, but two specific facts simultaneously is much harder. Fails under naive-truncate-16; passes under full-history.

turns.content: Front-loading two important details I'll come back to: I'm working on a commercial-lease dispute for the client Northwind Trading Ltd, and the court deadline for filing the response is 17 June 2026. Mostly random other stuff first.
Logged — Northwind Trading Ltd, response deadline 17 June 2026. Noted. What's first?
Random — best film soundtrack of the last decade?
Mica Levi's *Under the Skin* is a strong contender. Jonny Greenwood's *Phantom Thread* if you want something stranger. Hildur Guðnadóttir's *Joker* did unusual work for a mainstream film.
Mica Levi, will revisit. Question — best way to read more long-form journalism without bookmark sprawl?
Pocket or Readwise Reader — both let you queue and tag. Readwise Reader is the more powerful option if you want highlights to flow into Notion or Obsidian later.
Readwise Reader. Cooking — quickest weeknight pasta that isn't tomato-based?
Cacio e pepe in 12 minutes if you have decent pecorino and pepper. Aglio e olio in 10 if you have garlic and olive oil. Both fail if you skimp on pasta water for the emulsion.
Cacio e pepe always. Travel — Lisbon or Porto for a long weekend?
Porto if it's your first; smaller, more concentrated, the wine cellars are unique. Lisbon if you want more variety and don't mind walking hills. Porto is the better single-weekend pick.
Porto, useful. Random — what's the proper temperature for white wine, actually?

Most white wines are over-chilled. 8–10°C for full whites (white Burgundy, oaked Chardonnay), 10–12°C for crisp whites (Sauvignon Blanc, Picpoul), 6–8°C for sparkling. Aim for 'cool but not painful to hold'.
Useful framing. Office — single tweak that improves Zoom call presence?
Camera at eye level, not laptop level. Tilting your laptop screen up to look at the camera is the difference between 'engaged' and 'looking at someone's nostrils'. Books or a stand.
Will fix. Tools — best digital pen for iPad note-taking?
Apple Pencil 2 (or USB-C version for the Air) is the only realistic answer. The third-party options drift in calibration after a few months.
Apple Pencil. Quick — espresso machine descaling, white vinegar or proper descaler?
Proper descaler. Vinegar can damage rubber seals over time and the smell takes weeks to fully leave the boiler. Urnex Cleancaf or the manufacturer's own descaler — annoying to buy, easy to use.
Will buy proper. Reading — book on stoicism that isn't the obvious ones?
Skip Meditations and the recent self-help wave. Try Pierre Hadot's *Philosophy as a Way of Life* — it places stoicism in the context of ancient practice rather than modern productivity.
Hadot — noted. Hardware — best small Bluetooth speaker for kitchen use?
Bose SoundLink Flex if you want it survive splash and a knock. JBL Flip 6 for a slightly cheaper alternative. The Sonos Roam is great if you also have a Sonos system; otherwise it's overkill.
Bose Flex. Cooking — a stock recipe that's actually worth the time?

Roast chicken stock: roast a chicken carcass plus aromatics at 200°C for 30 minutes, then simmer 4 hours. The roast step is what makes the difference between supermarket-stock-tier and restaurant-tier.
Will try. Drinks — best mocktail for a non-drinker at a wedding?
Lyre's Spirits + a decent tonic, with cucumber. Or Seedlip Garden 108 if you want something more herbal. The trick is the tonic — boring tonic ruins both.
Lyre's, noted. Music — earphones for the gym under £150 that don't fall out?
Sony WF-1000XM5 if you want premium and ANC for treadmill running. Beats Fit Pro have wing tips and stay put for any movement. Avoid AirPods Pro for serious gym use — they slip during heavy lifts.
Beats Fit Pro. Quick — a houseplant for a bathroom with no natural light?
ZZ plant is the most tolerant. Snake plant (sansevieria) is second. Both survive weeks of low light and irregular watering — bathroom-tier neglect is fine.
ZZ plant. Reading — the best biography of a 20th-century scientist?
*The Strangest Man* by Graham Farmelo (Dirac), *Genius* by James Gleick (Feynman), or *Rosalind Franklin* by Brenda Maddox. Three different shapes of genius profiled with care.
Farmelo, noted. Quick one — a card game for two adults, plays in 15 minutes, more strategic than rummy?
Lost Cities, 7 Wonders Duel, or Patchwork. All three are designed for two players (rather than retro-fitted) and each plays in roughly 15–20 minutes. 7 Wonders Duel has the highest ceiling.
OK back to the case. Quickly — for the matter I mentioned at the start, can you remind me of both the client name and the filing deadline? Need to write them on the file cover.
